linux-gfx90a.blocked_reason: PORTED (fork bec85ee, real gfx90a code object). The libcudf.so x86-64 relocation SCALE wall is RESOLVED by --offload-compress (NOT a split, NOT -mcmodel=large): the HIP offload bundle is uncompressed by default, so each rocPRIM reduce TU embeds a ~159 MB device fatbin; --offload-compress (documented ROCm 7.2.1 clang flag) stores it CCOB-compressed and the runtime decompresses on load. Measured A/B on reductions/sum.cu: object 207.8 MB -> 52.1 MB (3.99x), .hip_fatbin 158.6 MB -> 2.89 MB (54.9x). The broad algorithm surface (reductions + aggregation + hash/sort groupby + hash/sort-merge join, ~44 new TUs) now links the SINGLE libcudf.so (~540 MB host image, well under 2 GiB; llvm-objdump --offloading shows 144 embedded code objects, ALL gfx90a, no multi-arch bloat). GPU 12/12 on MI250X (GCD 0): the 8-test baseline (no regression) + ReduceSumMinMaxProduct (int32), ReduceFloatingMean (float/double), HashInnerJoin (cuco static_multimap), HashGroupbySum (hash groupby). Found+fixed a real reduction correctness bug: cast_functor.cuh cast_fn's same-type overload returned T&& (a reference into expired transform_iterator storage that rocPRIM block-loaded as garbage -> min/max returned the seeded identity, double sum returned nan); the bug appeared exactly when OutputType==ElementType. Fix: return by value (UB on CUDA too; nvcc tolerated it). 17 cudf-internal symbols remain undefined: the 2 binary_operation overloads (jitify->hipRTC JIT, a documented follow-on) + 15 non-JIT modules not yet in the source list (quantile/tdigest/repeat/tile/lists::distinct/strings+structs scan_inclusive) -- none structural. src/io (nvCOMP/KvikIO) stays scoped out. See notes.md + PORTING_GUIDE.
